phishing_sms.summary_template: Possible phishing SMS campaign reported by {count} user(s) in {neighborhood}.
phishing_sms.explanation: These messages typically impersonate trusted organizations and include links to fake websites designed to steal personal information.
actions: Do not click any links in the message.
Do not reply to the sender.
Block the sending number.
Report the message to your carrier by forwarding it to 7726 (SPAM).
If you clicked a link, change any passwords you may have entered and monitor your accounts.

phishing_email.summary_template: Possible phishing email campaign reported by {count} user(s) in {neighborhood}.
phishing_email.explanation: These emails often mimic legitimate services and create urgency to trick recipients into revealing credentials or payment details.
actions: Do not click links or download attachments from the email.
Check the sender address carefully for misspellings.
Verify the claim by contacting the organization directly through their official website.
Mark the email as phishing or spam in your email client.
If you entered credentials, change your password immediately and enable MFA.

scam_call.summary_template: Possible scam call activity reported by {count} user(s) in {neighborhood}.
scam_call.explanation: Scam callers often impersonate government agencies, banks, or service providers and pressure victims into making immediate payments.
actions: Hang up immediately if pressured for payment or personal information.
Do not call back numbers left in voicemails.
Verify the caller's claim by contacting the organization through its official number.
Register your number on the National Do Not Call Registry.
Report the call to the FTC at reportfraud.ftc.gov.

account_compromise.summary_template: Possible account compromise affecting {count} user(s) in {neighborhood}.
account_compromise.explanation: Unauthorized access to accounts may result from credential theft, data breaches, or weak passwords.
actions: Change your password immediately on the affected account.
Enable multi-factor authentication (MFA) if available.
Check for unauthorized activity such as sent emails or purchases.
Run a security scan on your devices.
Contact the service provider's support team to report the compromise.

local_breach_alert.summary_template: Data breach reported affecting users in {neighborhood}.
local_breach_alert.explanation: A local business or service has disclosed a data breach that may have exposed personal information.
actions: Review the breach notification for details on what data was exposed.
Change passwords for any accounts associated with the breached service.
Monitor your financial statements for unauthorized charges.
Consider placing a fraud alert or credit freeze with major credit bureaus.
Watch for follow-up phishing attempts that reference the breach.

fraud_report.summary_template: Possible fraud activity reported by {count} user(s) in {neighborhood}.
fraud_report.explanation: In-person or online fraud attempts may target personal information, financial details, or identity documents.
actions: Do not share personal information with unverified individuals.
Ask for official identification and verify it independently.
Report the incident to local law enforcement.
File a report with the FTC at reportfraud.ftc.gov.
Alert neighbors through trusted community channels.

unknown.summary_template: Unclassified report(s) submitted by {count} user(s) in {neighborhood}.
unknown.explanation: This report does not match a known threat category. It may require manual review to determine its nature.
actions: Review the report details for any identifying information.
Do not take action on suspicious requests until verified.
If the report seems related to a known threat type, update its category.
When in doubt, consult official sources before responding.
